sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: 1. FSV Mainz 05 have struggled recently, with only 1 win in their last 5 matches, showing inconsistency with a record of 1-2-2. VfL Wolfsburg, on the other hand, have also had a mixed run with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses in their last 5, including a heavy 8-1 defeat to Bayern Munich. Both teams have had defensive issues, with Mainz conceding in 3 of their last 5 and Wolfsburg failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 12 matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, matches between Mainz and Wolfsburg have ended in draws (1-1 and 2-2), with both teams scoring in each game. Historically, these fixtures have been high-scoring, with over 2.5 goals in 8 of their last 10 meetings, suggesting an open contest is likely today.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Mainz sit 17th in the Bundesliga with just 12 points and a poor defensive record, conceding 31 goals this season (11th worst in the league). Wolfsburg, positioned 12th with 19 points, have scored 27 goals but conceded 38, highlighting defensive frailties. Mainz’s strength lies in interceptions and clearances per game (both league-leading), while Wolfsburg’s attacking threat could exploit Mainz’s low possession stats (45%, 16th in the league).

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Mainz will miss key midfielder Nadiem Amiri, who has 5 goals this season, potentially impacting their creativity. Wolfsburg’s Dženan Pejčinović has been in form with 5 goals in his last 5 matches, posing a significant threat, while Patrick Wimmer adds dynamism with 2 goals and 2 assists recently. Wolfsburg’s missing captain Maximilian Arnold could affect their midfield stability.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Wolfsburg’s slight edge in form and attacking output, coupled with Mainz’s struggles at home and in the standings, the Sofascore prediction of a 42% chance for an away win seems plausible. Both teams’ tendency to concede and score in recent head-to-heads points to a game with goals. Expect a tight match, but Wolfsburg may capitalize on Mainz’s defensive vulnerabilities.
